ORDER (EX PARTE) SHORTENING TIME RE: MOTION FOR LEAVE TO FILE A CROSS COMPLAINT (FILE: 8/28, OPP: 9/9, REPLY WAIVED, HEARING 9/13)

CROSS COMPLAINT FILED BY CROSS COMPLAINANT O'BRIEN, MICHAEL AN INDIVIDUAL O'BRIEN, JOE AN INDIVIDUAL ROES 1-10, INCLUSIVE AS TO CROSS DEFENDANT KEHOE, JIM KEHOE, JOHN PETRIN, MARY ROES 1-20, INCLUSIVE

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS MICHAEL O'BRIEN AND JOE O'BRIEN'S MOTION FOR LEAVE TO FILE CROSS-COMPLAINT IS GRANTED. DEFENDANTS HAVE ESTABLISHED THAT THE PROPOSED CROSS-COMPLAINT IS COMPULSORY, AS IT ARISES FROM THE SAME SERIES OF TRANSACTIONS OR OCCURRENCES AS THE CLAIMS BROUGHT AGAINST THEM IN PLAINTIFFS' COMPLAINT. DEFENDANTS DID NOT ACT IN BAD FAITH BY SEEKING LEAVE AT THIS STAGE IN THE LAWSUIT; THEY RECEIVED THE DMV INVESTIGATION FILE ON JULY 22, 2013, AND FILED THE PRESENT MOTION ON AUGUST 28, 2013. (DECLARATION OF PATRICK IN SUPPORT OF DEFENDANTS' MOTION FOR LEAVE TO FILE CROSS-COMPLAINT, 2-5.) PLAINTIFFS HAVE NOT PRODUCED ANY EVIDENCE TO SUPPORT THEIR ARGUMENT THAT DEFENDANTS KNEW OF THE DMV INVESTIGATION FILE, AND ITS FINDINGS, AT THE TIME DEFENDANTS FILED THEIR ANSWER. BECAUSE THE CURRENT TRIAL DATE IS SEPTEMBER 30, 2013, IT MUST BE CONTINUED TO ALLOW PLAINTIFF TO RESPOND TO THE PROPOSED CROSS-COMPLAINT. THE TRIAL DATE IS CONTINUED TO JANUARY 21, 2014 AT 9:30 A.M. THE DISCOVERY CUT-OFF WILL BE BASED ON THE NEW TRIAL DATE. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY C. VAUGHN, CSR # 6185 =302/MJM

Off Calendar Sep-23-2013 Off Calendar

MOTION FOR LEAVE TO To File X-Complt
Off Calendar Sep-13-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. G. RYAN PATRICK OF THE WIEGEL LAW GROUP, TEL. NO. 552-8230 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANTS;
NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER.

RULING - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS MICHAEL O'BRIEN and JOE O'BRIEN's MOTION FOR LEAVE TO File Cross-Complaint is granted. Defendants have established that the proposed cross-complaint is compulsory, as it arises from the same series of transactions or occurrences as the claims brought against them in Plaintiffs' complaint. Defendants did not act in bad faith by seeking leave at this stage in the lawsuit; they received the DMV Investigation file on July 22, 2013, and filed the present motion on August 28, 2013. (Declaration of Patrick In Support of Defendants' Motion for Leave to File Cross-Complaint, 2-5.) Plaintiffs have not produced any evidence to support their argument that Defendants knew of the DMV Investigation File, and its findings, at the time Defendants filed their answer. Because the current trial date is September 30, 2013, it must be continued to allow plaintiff to respond to the proposed cross-complaint. The trial date is continued to January 21, 2014 at 9:30 a.m. The discovery cut-off will be based on the new trial date. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185 =302/MJM

MINI MINUTES: No appearance on this matter.
RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF JIM KEHOE'S Motion For Preliminary Injunction. Denied without prejudice to being re-filed if it is supported by admissible evidence. The motion is not now supported by any admissible evidence and thus must be summarily denied on that ground alone.
